name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarships for College and University Students
description: Merit-cum-means scholarship that supports eligible Class XII graduates pursuing regular higher education in recognised institutions.

benefit.description: INR 12,000 annually for the first three years of graduation; higher rates apply to professional courses in later years under scheme norms.

other_conditions: Must be above the 80th percentile in Class XII and enrolled in a regular course at a recognised institution.

application.deadline: Check the current National Scholarship Portal schedule

non_financial: Direct benefit transfer to bank account

ai_metadata.estimated_processing_time: Subject to institute and state verification

ai_explanation.summary: Financial support for meritorious students from families with annual income up to INR 4.5 lakh.
ai_explanation.eligibility_reason_template: Confirm Class XII merit rank, family income, and regular-course enrolment on the National Scholarship Portal.
common_rejection_reasons: Not enrolled in a regular recognised course
tips: Keep the bank account seeded with Aadhaar where required.
next_steps: Register on the National Scholarship Portal.
Submit verified academic and income documents.

source.organization: Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education

name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
description: Scholarship for meritorious students from economically weaker families to help them continue secondary and higher-secondary education.

benefit.description: Annual scholarship for selected students from Class IX, renewable through Class XII subject to scheme conditions.

other_conditions: Regular Class VIII students in government, government-aided, or local-body schools must qualify the State or UT selection test.

application.deadline: Check the current State or UT NMMS notification

non_financial: Renewal support through Class XII, subject to conditions

ai_metadata.estimated_processing_time: State or UT selection and verification

ai_explanation.summary: Supports economically weaker, meritorious school students at risk of dropping out after Class VIII.
ai_explanation.eligibility_reason_template: Check your State or UT NMMS test notification, school type, marks, and family-income limit.
tips: Ask your school for the State or UT NMMS application schedule.
next_steps: Confirm the selection-test schedule with your school.

source.organization: Department of School Education and Literacy, Ministry of Education

name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship for Girls
description: Scholarship that encourages eligible girl students to pursue technical diploma and degree programmes at AICTE-approved institutions.

benefit.description: Up to INR 50,000 per annum for each year of study, subject to AICTE and National Scholarship Portal guidelines.

other_conditions: Must be admitted to the first year of an AICTE-approved technical degree or diploma course; family-size and other AICTE conditions apply.

application.deadline: Check the current National Scholarship Portal schedule

ai_metadata.estimated_processing_time: Subject to institute and AICTE verification

ai_explanation.summary: Technical-education scholarship for eligible girl students admitted to AICTE-approved programmes.
ai_explanation.eligibility_reason_template: Confirm AICTE approval, first-year admission, family income, and current scheme conditions.
common_rejection_reasons: Institution or course is not AICTE-approved
tips: Obtain an institute-verified admission record before applying.
next_steps: Verify the course with your institute.
Apply through the National Scholarship Portal.

source.organization: All India Council for Technical Education

name: PM YASASVI Scholarship
subcategory: Scholarship for OBC, EBC and DNT Students
description: Umbrella scholarship scheme supporting eligible OBC, Economically Backward Class, and Denotified Tribe students through pre-matric, post-matric, and top-class education components.

benefit.description: Scholarship, tuition support, and related assistance vary by the applicable PM YASASVI sub-scheme and course level.

other_conditions: Applicant must meet the category, income, institution, and course conditions of the applicable PM YASASVI component.

application.deadline: Check the current National Scholarship Portal schedule

non_financial: Support for school and higher education

ai_metadata.estimated_processing_time: Subject to document and institute verification

ai_explanation.summary: Education support for eligible OBC, EBC, and DNT students under the PM YASASVI umbrella scheme.
ai_explanation.eligibility_reason_template: Confirm the applicable PM YASASVI component, category certificate, income limit, and institution requirements.
tips: Use the exact category and income documents accepted by your State or UT.
frequently_asked_questions: Which PM YASASVI component applies to me?
next_steps: Review PM YASASVI details on the official portal.

source.organization: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment
